quiz_questions.hint: Think about butter and flour. Which sugar has lower moisture?
quiz_questions.feedback_text: Correct! High butter increases spreading. White sugar creates a crisp texture. Low flour keeps the cookie thin and flat.

quiz_questions.hint: Use ingredients that reduce spreading and add moisture.
quiz_questions.feedback_text: Correct! Brown sugar adds moisture and chewiness. More flour gives the cookie structure. Less butter reduces spreading.

quiz_questions.prompt: Oh no! My cookies came out too soft :(, but I wanted them crispier. What should I change?
quiz_questions.feedback_text: Correct! Yay you saved my cookies!

quiz_questions.prompt: Oh no! My cookies spread too much and came out flat. I wanted them thick and chewy. What should I change?
choices: Add more flour and use brown sugar
Increase butter and use white sugar
Use white sugar and reduce flour
quiz_questions.correct_answer: Add more flour and use brown sugar
quiz_questions.feedback_text: Correct! I am so grateful for you, have a cookie please :)

quiz_end_page.title: COMPLETE
quiz_end_page.message: HAVE A COOKIE & A GOOD DAY!
